title: Post-Market Monitoring, Information Sharing and Market Surveillance

articles.title: Post-Market Monitoring
articles.summary: Providers establish post-market monitoring systems proportionate to nature and risks. Plan must be part of technical documentation.

articles.title: Reporting of Serious Incidents
articles.summary: Report serious incidents to market surveillance authorities immediately and no later than 15 days.

articles.title: Market Surveillance and Control
articles.summary: Authorities carry out checks including access to training data, source code, and documentation.

articles.title: Mutual Assistance and GPAI Control
articles.summary: AI Office enforces GPAI obligations. Member States may request investigation of GPAI infringements.

articles.title: Supervision of Real-World Testing
articles.summary: Authorities oversee testing and may require changes, suspension, or termination.

articles.title: Fundamental Rights Authorities
articles.summary: National fundamental rights authorities may request documentation and access logs.

articles.title: Confidentiality
articles.summary: Respect for confidentiality, business secrets, and personal data protection.

articles.title: AI Systems Presenting Risk — National Level
articles.summary: Authorities evaluate and require corrective actions for AI systems presenting risk.

articles.title: Non-Compliant AI Systems
articles.summary: Corrective action requirements for non-compliant systems; notification to Commission and Member States.

articles.title: Union Safeguard Procedure
articles.summary: Commission evaluates national measures. If justified, all Member States take similar measures.

articles.title: Compliant Systems Presenting Risk
articles.summary: Procedure for compliant systems that nevertheless present risk to health, safety, or fundamental rights.

articles.title: Formal Non-Compliance
articles.summary: Corrective actions for formal non-compliance (missing CE marking, documentation) without substantive risk.

articles.title: Union AI Testing Support
articles.summary: Commission may designate testing facilities for market surveillance support.

articles.title: Right to Explanation
articles.summary: Affected persons have right to clear, meaningful explanations of AI's role in decisions.

articles.title: Right to Complain
articles.summary: Natural and legal persons may lodge complaints about non-compliance with market surveillance authority.

articles.title: Whistleblower Protection
articles.summary: Channels for reporting infringements with whistleblower protection per Directive 2019/1937.

articles.title: Request for Investigation
articles.summary: Market surveillance authorities may request AI Office to investigate GPAI infringements.

articles.title: AI Office Investigation Powers
articles.summary: AI Office may request information, conduct evaluations, and require corrective actions from GPAI providers.

articles.title: Alert and Model Evaluation
articles.summary: Scientific panel alerts AI Office about GPAI risks, triggering evaluation and potential systemic risk classification.

articles.title: Documentation and Information Requests
articles.summary: AI Office may request all documentation including training methodology, data sources, compute details.

articles.title: Power to Conduct Evaluations
articles.summary: AI Office may evaluate GPAI models for compliance, including adversarial testing with independent experts.

articles.title: Power to Request Measures
articles.summary: AI Office may require corrective actions, restrict availability, or withdraw GPAI models from market.

articles.title: Procedural Rights
articles.summary: GPAI providers have rights to be heard, access file, and receive reasoned decisions.
